rejection.reason: R2 fired: the proposed kernel is a CPDAG/Markov-equivalence orientation certificate for counterfactual and path-specific fairness. The evidence located in Track B places the topic in structural causal model counterfactual fairness, path-specific/cross-world mediation identification, and graphical-model equivalence-class reasoning rather than the allowed econometric identification, partial-identification, panel, or causal-ML orthogonality clusters. The recent CPDAG fairness paper confirms that this is framed as counterfactual fairness under imperfect causal graphs, while the path-specific anchors rely on directed-path nested counterfactuals and recanting-witness style graphical criteria.

evidence_refs.claim: Kusner, Loftus, Russell, and Silva introduce counterfactual fairness as a causal-model fairness definition based on protected-attribute counterfactual worlds.
evidence_refs.author: Kusner, Loftus, Russell, and Silva

evidence_refs.claim: Chiappa frames path-specific counterfactual fairness as disregarding effects along unfair causal pathways in a specified causal model.
evidence_refs.author: Chiappa

evidence_refs.claim: Su et al. explicitly target counterfactual fairness under imperfect causal graphs such as CPDAGs, with identifiability criteria and bounds under incomplete causal knowledge.
evidence_refs.author: Su, Tan, Domeniconi, Cui, Wang, and Yu

evidence_refs.claim: Avin, Shpitser, and Pearl give graphical criteria for path-specific effects, tying the relevant obstruction to directed paths and recanting-witness style structure.
evidence_refs.author: Avin, Shpitser, and Pearl

suggested_directions: Re-run as an ordinary MPDAG/CPDAG total-effect invariance problem for P(Y | do(A)) over DAG completions, dropping fairness and path-specific counterfactual semantics.
Re-run on an econometric partial-identification gap from the study bundle, such as inference for nonparametric bound intersections in the Manski/Imbens partial-ID cluster.
If field-tier ML fairness is acceptable, re-run explicitly as a fairness-theory topic anchored to Su et al. 2026 rather than as a CausalSmith econometric-identification proposal.

literature_map:
1. Kusner, Loftus, Russell, and Silva (2017, NeurIPS) introduce counterfactual fairness as protected-attribute counterfactual invariance in a causal model.
2. Chiappa (2019, AAAI) defines path-specific counterfactual fairness by retaining fair pathways and disregarding unfair pathways.
3. Zhang and Bareinboim (2018, AAAI) decompose disparity into direct, indirect, and spurious counterfactual mechanisms.
4. Nabi and Shpitser (2018, AAAI) formulate discrimination as impermissible path-specific effects using nested counterfactuals.
5. Avin, Shpitser, and Pearl (2005, IJCAI) give graphical path-specific-effect identification criteria, including the recanting-witness obstruction.
6. Shpitser and Pearl (2008, JMLR) place counterfactual identification in the causal hierarchy with graphical algorithms and impossibility characterizations.
7. Perkovic (2020, UAI/PMLR) gives total-effect identification criteria in MPDAGs/CPDAGs, but not path-specific fairness.
8. Zuo et al. (2022, NeurIPS) study counterfactual fairness with partially known causal graphs through ancestral-relation conditions.
9. Su et al. (2026, AAAI) directly studies counterfactual fairness under CPDAG uncertainty and avoids exhaustive DAG enumeration for ordinary counterfactual fairness criteria/bounds.
10. Valerio et al. (2026, arXiv/BIAS workshop) audits counterfactual fairness under graph uncertainty by aggregating plausible DAGs and confidence bounds.
11. The located literature confirms the user-specified live gap: path-specific unfair effects are not given an if-and-only-if CPDAG invariance certificate.
12. The same literature also confirms R2: the gap lives in SCM counterfactual fairness, path-specific/cross-world mediation ID, and graphical equivalence-class theory, which this stage excludes from CausalSmith pass-forward scope.
prior_proposal_map:
Active research/eid_cf_fairness_cpdag_witness currently contains only state/pipeline metadata; no prior proposal tex or reviewer JSON for the same qid was available.
Exact keyword search found no prior CausalSmith artifact for counterfactual fairness, recanting witnesses, CPDAG orientation, protected attributes, or DAG-invariant fairness estimands.
The closest Track-A exact-ID substrate is downgraded eid_data_fusion_k_source_v1: its proposal and reviewers flag source-minimal witness certificates beyond generic SS-ID/do-search outputs.
Its reviewer JSONs angle0_v1 and angle0_v2 yielded strengthening_paths about preserved source laws, overlap-hedge witnesses, and a three-source binary boundary template.
The closest path-specific/natural-effect substrate is failed pid_mediation_proxy_v1: it contains an explicit NOT YET PROVED sharp joint PNDE/TNIE proxy-null line-segment conjecture and reviewer requests for LP face conditions.
A second adjacent mediation substrate is downgraded eid_frontdoor_multi_mediator_v1: it contains an unproved hidden-mediator coupling/proxy incompatibility conjecture with reviewer requests for a rational interior-active-face witness.
Bank READMEs were mostly metadata/TODO stubs for these entries and added little beyond the proposal/reviewer files.
The supplied Study Pipeline Gaps were IV/Manski/interval-regression oriented and did not add topic-relevant prior-proposal evidence for this Track A scan.
